title: RAG, Long Context, Fine-Tuning, Memory, and Architecture Evolution · Code Recall
summary: A delayed retrieval review reconstructs rag, long context, fine-tuning, memory, and architecture evolution from source, document, chunk, model, index, query, candidate, response, citation, evaluation, operations, and stakeholder evidence, then attacks one retained misconception. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for evidence-based architecture and migration record after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugg…
objectives: Compare search, RAG, full-context, cached-context, and hierarchical approaches on corpus size, freshness, authorization, position, latency, cost, citations, and quality.
Use retrieval for external mutable evidence and fine-tuning for evaluated behavior or domain adaptation while testing combinations and update cost.
Evaluate corrective retrieval, self-reflection, verification, query rewriting, and fallback against deterministic stage evidence and false confidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A review RAG engineering team handles case 3652 during a public-benefits policy change. Build deterministic Python evidence to compare RAG, long context, fine-tuning, memory, graph, and self-corrective designs before migration; preserve source provenance, tenant authorization, deletion, freshness, citation offsets, and untrusted-data separation; then replace one exact identifier with a synonym and compare lexical, dense, and hybrid recall. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Change parsers, chunkers, embeddings, indexes, rerankers, prompts, models, providers, policies, and schemas through compatibility matrices, canaries, dual runs, rollback, and deletion.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: A framework abstraction makes RAG component migration automatic.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Change parsers, chunkers, embeddings, indexes, rerankers, prompts, models, providers, policies, and schemas through compatibility matrices, canaries, dual runs, rollback, and deletion. Artifact under review: evidence-based architecture and migration record.

options.text: The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.

For separate evidence from confidence, run one convenient Rag Architecture Migration Agility query, omit corpus and version identity, relevance judgments, tenant policy, changed documents, abstention, citations, failure and recovery, then accept a plausible answer as RAG proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Rag Architecture Migration Agility, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Change parsers, chunkers, embeddings, indexes, rerankers, prompts, models, providers, policies, and schemas through compatibility matrices, canaries, dual runs, rollback, and deletion.
